# Security Standards

> Generated by Velocity /init. Customize for your team.
> Referenced by: Security Agent, engineer guardrail hooks.

## Authentication and Authorization

- Never roll your own auth. Use established libraries and identity providers.
- All API endpoints must be authenticated unless explicitly designed as public.
- Authorization checks must be in the application layer, not only at the API gateway.
- Principle of least privilege: users and services get only what they need.

## Secrets Management

- No secrets in source code. No secrets in `.env` files committed to git.
- Use a secrets manager (AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, GCP Secret Manager, etc.).
- Rotate secrets on a defined schedule. Rotate immediately if exposed.
- CI/CD pipelines must use injected secrets, not stored credentials.

## Input Validation

- Validate and sanitize all inputs at system boundaries.
- Reject unexpected data formats early — do not pass invalid inputs deeper.
- Parameterize all database queries. No string concatenation in SQL.
- Validate file uploads: type, size, content.

## Data Protection

- Classify data by sensitivity: PII, financial, health, internal, public.
- Encrypt PII and sensitive data at rest and in transit.
- Do not log PII. Mask or hash in logs if reference is required.
- Apply data retention policies.

## Dependencies

- Scan all dependencies for known vulnerabilities before adding (`npm audit`, `snyk`, `dependabot`).
- Pin dependency versions. Do not use `latest` or `*` ranges in production.
- Review major dependency upgrades for security notes.

## Security Reviews

- Run `/security-review` skill for any feature that: handles authentication, processes payments, stores PII, or exposes a new public API endpoint.
- Threat model new features before implementation when they touch trust boundaries.

## Compliance

> Add applicable compliance requirements here: HIPAA, PCI-DSS, SOC2, GDPR, ISO27001, etc.
